name: Cabernet Sauvignon
description: The undisputed king of red wines, producing full-bodied wines with blackcurrant, cedar, and spice notes. It thrives in warm climates and forms the backbone of Bordeaux's legendary blends.

grapeCard.name: Cabernet Sauvignon
grapeCard.info: The undisputed king of red wines, producing full-bodied wines with blackcurrant, cedar, and spice notes. It thrives in warm climates and forms the backbone of Bordeaux's legendary blends.

name: Pinot Noir
description: Known as the heartbreak grape for its difficulty to grow, it produces elegant wines with cherry and earthy notes. This thin-skinned variety expresses terroir like no other grape.

grapeCard.name: Pinot Noir
grapeCard.info: Known as the heartbreak grape for its difficulty to grow, it produces elegant wines with cherry and earthy notes. This thin-skinned variety expresses terroir like no other grape.

name: Chardonnay
description: The world's most versatile white grape, acting as a canvas for winemakers to craft everything from crisp to buttery styles. It responds beautifully to oak aging and malolactic fermentation.

grapeCard.name: Chardonnay
grapeCard.info: The world's most versatile white grape, acting as a canvas for winemakers to craft everything from crisp to buttery styles. It responds beautifully to oak aging and malolactic fermentation.

name: Merlot
description: A soft and velvety red grape that produces approachable wines with plum and chocolate flavors. It serves as an excellent blending partner and stands beautifully on its own.

grapeCard.name: Merlot
grapeCard.info: A soft and velvety red grape that produces approachable wines with plum and chocolate flavors. It serves as an excellent blending partner and stands beautifully on its own.

name: Syrah / Shiraz
description: A powerful grape producing intense wines with pepper, smoke, and dark fruit characteristics. Known as Syrah in France and Shiraz in Australia, it adapts dramatically to its climate.

grapeCard.name: Syrah / Shiraz
grapeCard.info: A powerful grape producing intense wines with pepper, smoke, and dark fruit characteristics. Known as Syrah in France and Shiraz in Australia, it adapts dramatically to its climate.

name: Sauvignon Blanc
description: A zesty and herbaceous white grape known for its crisp acidity and distinctive citrus and green notes. It expresses vastly different characters from the Loire Valley to New Zealand.

grapeCard.name: Sauvignon Blanc
grapeCard.info: A zesty and herbaceous white grape known for its crisp acidity and distinctive citrus and green notes. It expresses vastly different characters from the Loire Valley to New Zealand.

name: Riesling
description: An aromatic white grape with exceptional purity and high acidity that ages remarkably well. It ranges from bone-dry to lusciously sweet, developing complex petrol notes with age.

grapeCard.name: Riesling
grapeCard.info: An aromatic white grape with exceptional purity and high acidity that ages remarkably well. It ranges from bone-dry to lusciously sweet, developing complex petrol notes with age.

name: Nebbiolo
description: A legendary Italian grape producing pale-colored wines with massive tannins and floral aromas of tar and roses. It requires long aging to soften and is the soul of Barolo and Barbaresco.

grapeCard.name: Nebbiolo
grapeCard.info: A legendary Italian grape producing pale-colored wines with massive tannins and floral aromas of tar and roses. It requires long aging to soften and is the soul of Barolo and Barbaresco.

name: Sangiovese
description: Italy's most planted red grape, producing savory wines with vibrant cherry and tomato leaf characters. It is the backbone of Chianti, Brunello di Montalcino, and countless Tuscan wines.

grapeCard.name: Sangiovese
grapeCard.info: Italy's most planted red grape, producing savory wines with vibrant cherry and tomato leaf characters. It is the backbone of Chianti, Brunello di Montalcino, and countless Tuscan wines.

name: Grenache
description: A sweet red fruit-driven grape that produces wines high in alcohol with strawberry and raspberry flavors. It is a key component in Southern Rhône blends and Spanish Garnacha wines.

grapeCard.name: Grenache
grapeCard.info: A sweet red fruit-driven grape that produces wines high in alcohol with strawberry and raspberry flavors. It is a key component in Southern Rhône blends and Spanish Garnacha wines.

name: Tempranillo
description: Spain's noble grape variety, producing elegant wines with leather, tobacco, and red berry notes. It is the backbone of Rioja and Ribera del Duero, aging beautifully in American oak.

grapeCard.name: Tempranillo
grapeCard.info: Spain's noble grape variety, producing elegant wines with leather, tobacco, and red berry notes. It is the backbone of Rioja and Ribera del Duero, aging beautifully in American oak.

name: Malbec
description: Originally from Cahors, France, this dark and inky grape has become Argentina's signature variety. It produces velvety wines with intense plum, blueberry, and cocoa flavors.

grapeCard.name: Malbec
grapeCard.info: Originally from Cahors, France, this dark and inky grape has become Argentina's signature variety. It produces velvety wines with intense plum, blueberry, and cocoa flavors.

name: Cabernet Franc
description: The parent grape of Cabernet Sauvignon, known for its herbaceous character with bell pepper and raspberry notes. It excels in the Loire Valley and plays a supporting role in Bordeaux blends.

grapeCard.name: Cabernet Franc
grapeCard.info: The parent grape of Cabernet Sauvignon, known for its herbaceous character with bell pepper and raspberry notes. It excels in the Loire Valley and plays a supporting role in Bordeaux blends.

name: Chenin Blanc
description: An incredibly versatile chameleon grape capable of producing dry, sweet, and sparkling wines. Its high acidity allows for exceptional aging and expression of terroir.

grapeCard.name: Chenin Blanc
grapeCard.info: An incredibly versatile chameleon grape capable of producing dry, sweet, and sparkling wines. Its high acidity allows for exceptional aging and expression of terroir.

name: Viognier
description: A perfumed and aromatic white grape producing full-bodied wines with peach, tangerine, and honeysuckle notes. It originates from the Rhône Valley and is often co-fermented with Syrah.

grapeCard.name: Viognier
grapeCard.info: A perfumed and aromatic white grape producing full-bodied wines with peach, tangerine, and honeysuckle notes. It originates from the Rhône Valley and is often co-fermented with Syrah.

name: Gamay
description: A fruity and fresh grape that produces light, gulpable wines perfect for serving slightly chilled. It is the sole grape of Beaujolais and excels with carbonic maceration techniques.

grapeCard.name: Gamay
grapeCard.info: A fruity and fresh grape that produces light, gulpable wines perfect for serving slightly chilled. It is the sole grape of Beaujolais and excels with carbonic maceration techniques.

name: Zinfandel
description: A bold and jammy grape that produces high-alcohol wines with ripe berry and spice characteristics. Genetically identical to Primitivo, it has become California's signature red variety.

grapeCard.name: Zinfandel
grapeCard.info: A bold and jammy grape that produces high-alcohol wines with ripe berry and spice characteristics. Genetically identical to Primitivo, it has become California's signature red variety.

name: Semillon
description: A golden-hued grape often blended with Sauvignon Blanc, producing waxy whites with lemon curd and lanolin notes. It is essential for Sauternes and ages remarkably well.

grapeCard.name: Semillon
grapeCard.info: A golden-hued grape often blended with Sauvignon Blanc, producing waxy whites with lemon curd and lanolin notes. It is essential for Sauternes and ages remarkably well.

name: Mourvèdre
description: A meaty and herbal red grape that produces full-bodied wines with game, black fruit, and herb flavors. It is a key blending partner in GSM blends and the star of Bandol rosé.

grapeCard.name: Mourvèdre
grapeCard.info: A meaty and herbal red grape that produces full-bodied wines with game, black fruit, and herb flavors. It is a key blending partner in GSM blends and the star of Bandol rosé.

name: Barbera
description: A juicy and high-acid Italian grape that produces everyday drinking wines with sour cherry and herb flavors. It thrives in Piedmont and offers excellent value.

grapeCard.name: Barbera
grapeCard.info: A juicy and high-acid Italian grape that produces everyday drinking wines with sour cherry and herb flavors. It thrives in Piedmont and offers excellent value.

name: Pinot Gris
description: A mutation of Pinot Noir that produces white wines ranging from zesty Italian Pinot Grigio to rich Alsatian styles. Its grayish-pink skin gives it its name.

grapeCard.name: Pinot Gris
grapeCard.info: A mutation of Pinot Noir that produces white wines ranging from zesty Italian Pinot Grigio to rich Alsatian styles. Its grayish-pink skin gives it its name.

name: Petit Verdot
description: A late-ripening Bordeaux blending grape that adds violet aromas and deep color to wines. Rarely bottled alone, it provides structure and inky depth to the final blend.

grapeCard.name: Petit Verdot
grapeCard.info: A late-ripening Bordeaux blending grape that adds violet aromas and deep color to wines. Rarely bottled alone, it provides structure and inky depth to the final blend.

name: Carmenère
description: Once thought extinct in Bordeaux, this lost grape found a new home in Chile where it produces distinctive wines. Its green pepper and raspberry notes make it Chile's signature variety.

grapeCard.name: Carmenère
grapeCard.info: Once thought extinct in Bordeaux, this lost grape found a new home in Chile where it produces distinctive wines. Its green pepper and raspberry notes make it Chile's signature variety.

name: Torrontés
description: Argentina's signature white grape that smells sweet but tastes dry, often deceiving the senses. Its intense rose and lychee aromas make it one of the world's most distinctive whites.

grapeCard.name: Torrontés
grapeCard.info: Argentina's signature white grape that smells sweet but tastes dry, often deceiving the senses. Its intense rose and lychee aromas make it one of the world's most distinctive whites.

name: Moscato
description: One of the world's oldest grape families, known for its sweet, fizzy, and intensely aromatic wines. Its orange blossom perfume and low alcohol make it perfect for casual sipping.

grapeCard.name: Moscato
grapeCard.info: One of the world's oldest grape families, known for its sweet, fizzy, and intensely aromatic wines. Its orange blossom perfume and low alcohol make it perfect for casual sipping.

name: Gewürztraminer
description: A highly aromatic grape with distinctive lychee and rose petal aromas that is unmistakable in blind tastings. Its name means 'spiced traminer' and it produces rich wines with low acidity.

grapeCard.name: Gewürztraminer
grapeCard.info: A highly aromatic grape with distinctive lychee and rose petal aromas that is unmistakable in blind tastings. Its name means 'spiced traminer' and it produces rich wines with low acidity.

name: Albariño
description: Spain's coastal Atlantic white grape that produces crisp, refreshing wines with apricot and sea salt notes. It thrives in Rías Baixas where ocean influence creates distinctive saline character.

grapeCard.name: Albariño
grapeCard.info: Spain's coastal Atlantic white grape that produces crisp, refreshing wines with apricot and sea salt notes. It thrives in Rías Baixas where ocean influence creates distinctive saline character.

name: Grüner Veltliner
description: Austria's pride and most planted grape, known for its distinctive white pepper and green pea notes. It ranges from light and crisp to powerful and age-worthy depending on terroir.

grapeCard.name: Grüner Veltliner
grapeCard.info: Austria's pride and most planted grape, known for its distinctive white pepper and green pea notes. It ranges from light and crisp to powerful and age-worthy depending on terroir.

name: Vermentino
description: A Mediterranean white grape that thrives along coastal regions, producing citrus-driven wines with herbal hints. It is known by different names across Sardinia, Corsica, and the French Riviera.

grapeCard.name: Vermentino
grapeCard.info: A Mediterranean white grape that thrives along coastal regions, producing citrus-driven wines with herbal hints. It is known by different names across Sardinia, Corsica, and the French Riviera.

name: Verdejo
description: An aromatic Spanish white grape that produces herbal wines with fennel and grapefruit characteristics. It is the star of Rueda and offers a Spanish alternative to Sauvignon Blanc.

grapeCard.name: Verdejo
grapeCard.info: An aromatic Spanish white grape that produces herbal wines with fennel and grapefruit characteristics. It is the star of Rueda and offers a Spanish alternative to Sauvignon Blanc.

name: Roussanne
description: A golden Rhône white grape that produces full-bodied wines with herbal tea and pear flavors. It is often blended with Marsanne and ages beautifully, developing honeyed complexity.

grapeCard.name: Roussanne
grapeCard.info: A golden Rhône white grape that produces full-bodied wines with herbal tea and pear flavors. It is often blended with Marsanne and ages beautifully, developing honeyed complexity.

name: Marsanne
description: A rich white Rhône grape that produces nutty, full-bodied wines with almond and marzipan notes. It develops waxy, honeyed flavors with age and pairs beautifully with rich dishes.

grapeCard.name: Marsanne
grapeCard.info: A rich white Rhône grape that produces nutty, full-bodied wines with almond and marzipan notes. It develops waxy, honeyed flavors with age and pairs beautifully with rich dishes.

name: Trebbiano
description: A light and neutral Italian workhorse grape that produces high-volume, everyday white wines. It forms the base of many Italian wines and French Cognac production.

grapeCard.name: Trebbiano
grapeCard.info: A light and neutral Italian workhorse grape that produces high-volume, everyday white wines. It forms the base of many Italian wines and French Cognac production.

name: Fiano
description: An aromatic Italian white grape from Campania that produces wines with hazelnut and honey notes. It has ancient origins and is experiencing a modern renaissance.

grapeCard.name: Fiano
grapeCard.info: An aromatic Italian white grape from Campania that produces wines with hazelnut and honey notes. It has ancient origins and is experiencing a modern renaissance.

name: Assyrtiko
description: A Greek grape from Santorini with intense mineral character and very high acidity. It thrives on volcanic soils and produces wines with remarkable saline and citrus notes.

grapeCard.name: Assyrtiko
grapeCard.info: A Greek grape from Santorini with intense mineral character and very high acidity. It thrives on volcanic soils and produces wines with remarkable saline and citrus notes.

name: Aglianico
description: A powerful Italian red grape from southern Italy producing tannic, age-worthy wines with plum and leather notes. It is often called the 'Barolo of the South'.

grapeCard.name: Aglianico
grapeCard.info: A powerful Italian red grape from southern Italy producing tannic, age-worthy wines with plum and leather notes. It is often called the 'Barolo of the South'.

name: Nero d'Avola
description: Sicily's most important red grape, producing rich wines with black cherry, plum, and spice flavors. It has become the ambassador of Sicilian winemaking.

grapeCard.name: Nero d'Avola
grapeCard.info: Sicily's most important red grape, producing rich wines with black cherry, plum, and spice flavors. It has become the ambassador of Sicilian winemaking.

name: Touriga Nacional
description: Portugal's noblest grape and the backbone of Port wine, producing intensely colored wines with violet and dark fruit notes. It excels in both fortified and dry table wines.

grapeCard.name: Touriga Nacional
grapeCard.info: Portugal's noblest grape and the backbone of Port wine, producing intensely colored wines with violet and dark fruit notes. It excels in both fortified and dry table wines.

name: Tannat
description: A tannic powerhouse grape that has become Uruguay's national variety. It produces deeply colored, robust wines with blackberry and leather characteristics.

grapeCard.name: Tannat
grapeCard.info: A tannic powerhouse grape that has become Uruguay's national variety. It produces deeply colored, robust wines with blackberry and leather characteristics.

name: Furmint
description: The primary grape of Hungary's famous Tokaji sweet wines with very high acidity and citrus notes. It also makes excellent dry wines with smoky, minerally complexity.

grapeCard.name: Furmint
grapeCard.info: The primary grape of Hungary's famous Tokaji sweet wines with very high acidity and citrus notes. It also makes excellent dry wines with smoky, minerally complexity.

name: Marselan
description: A Cabernet Sauvignon and Grenache crossing that has become the flagship red of modern China. Produces plush, dark-fruited wines with polished tannins in the foothills of Helan Mountain.

grapeCard.name: Marselan
grapeCard.info: A Cabernet Sauvignon and Grenache crossing that has become the flagship red of modern China. Produces plush, dark-fruited wines with polished tannins in the foothills of Helan Mountain.

name: Cabernet Gernischt
description: A historic Bordeaux transplant thriving in China, often mistaken for Carmenère. Shows herbal spice and black fruit with a distinctly savory edge in cooler Chinese vineyards.

grapeCard.name: Cabernet Gernischt
grapeCard.info: A historic Bordeaux transplant thriving in China, often mistaken for Carmenère. Shows herbal spice and black fruit with a distinctly savory edge in cooler Chinese vineyards.

name: Koshu
description: Japan's signature pale-purple skinned grape that yields delicate, mineral-driven whites. Its subtle citrus and jasmine notes shine in the misty foothills of Mount Fuji.

grapeCard.name: Koshu
grapeCard.info: Japan's signature pale-purple skinned grape that yields delicate, mineral-driven whites. Its subtle citrus and jasmine notes shine in the misty foothills of Mount Fuji.

name: Muscat Bailey A
description: A hybrid created in Japan combining Muscat of Hamburg and Bailey, producing aromatic, softly tannic reds with strawberry candy notes. Often vinified as a chilled, vibrant style.

grapeCard.name: Muscat Bailey A
grapeCard.info: A hybrid created in Japan combining Muscat of Hamburg and Bailey, producing aromatic, softly tannic reds with strawberry candy notes. Often vinified as a chilled, vibrant style.

name: Saperavi
description: Georgia's flagship teinturier red with intense black fruit, savory spice, and the structure to age gracefully.

grapeCard.name: Saperavi
grapeCard.info: Georgia's flagship teinturier red with intense black fruit, savory spice, and the structure to age gracefully.

name: Rkatsiteli
description: Ancient Georgian white grape famed for amber qvevri wines with quince, tea, and honeyed depth.

grapeCard.name: Rkatsiteli
grapeCard.info: Ancient Georgian white grape famed for amber qvevri wines with quince, tea, and honeyed depth.

name: Kisi
description: Fragrant Georgian white offering apricot, white flowers, and subtle tannin when fermented on skins.

grapeCard.name: Kisi
grapeCard.info: Fragrant Georgian white offering apricot, white flowers, and subtle tannin when fermented on skins.

name: Dolcetto
description: A Piedmontese red grape known for juicy black fruit, low acidity, and soft tannins, making it ideal for early drinking. Despite the name meaning 'little sweet one,' the wines are typically dry and pleasantly bitter on the finish.

grapeCard.name: Dolcetto
grapeCard.info: A Piedmontese red grape known for juicy black fruit, low acidity, and soft tannins, making it ideal for early drinking. Despite the name meaning 'little sweet one,' the wines are typically dry and pleasantly bitter on the finish.

name: Pinotage
description: South Africa's signature crossing of Pinot Noir and Cinsault, producing deeply colored wines with dark berry fruit, smoke, and earthy spice. Styles range from fresh and fruit-driven to concentrated, oak-aged bottlings.

grapeCard.name: Pinotage
grapeCard.info: South Africa's signature crossing of Pinot Noir and Cinsault, producing deeply colored wines with dark berry fruit, smoke, and earthy spice. Styles range from fresh and fruit-driven to concentrated, oak-aged bottlings.

name: Montepulciano
description: A widely planted central Italian red grape that gives plush, fruit-forward wines with moderate tannin and herbal depth. It is the backbone of Montepulciano d'Abruzzo and responds well to both youthful and oak-aged styles.

grapeCard.name: Montepulciano
grapeCard.info: A widely planted central Italian red grape that gives plush, fruit-forward wines with moderate tannin and herbal depth. It is the backbone of Montepulciano d'Abruzzo and responds well to both youthful and oak-aged styles.

name: Corvina
description: The principal red grape of Valpolicella, prized for sour-cherry brightness and herbal lift. Its thick skins and naturally high acidity make it especially suited to appassimento styles like Amarone.

grapeCard.name: Corvina
grapeCard.info: The principal red grape of Valpolicella, prized for sour-cherry brightness and herbal lift. Its thick skins and naturally high acidity make it especially suited to appassimento styles like Amarone.

name: Rondinella
description: A reliable blending grape from Veneto commonly paired with Corvina in Valpolicella and Amarone. It contributes color, floral aromatics, and steady acidity, especially in dried-grape wines.

grapeCard.name: Rondinella
grapeCard.info: A reliable blending grape from Veneto commonly paired with Corvina in Valpolicella and Amarone. It contributes color, floral aromatics, and steady acidity, especially in dried-grape wines.

name: Pinot Blanc
description: A white mutation of Pinot Noir producing crisp wines with orchard fruit, almond, and gentle floral notes. It shines in Alsace, Alto Adige, and Germany, where styles range from fresh and linear to richer, lees-aged expressions.

grapeCard.name: Pinot Blanc
grapeCard.info: A white mutation of Pinot Noir producing crisp wines with orchard fruit, almond, and gentle floral notes. It shines in Alsace, Alto Adige, and Germany, where styles range from fresh and linear to richer, lees-aged expressions.

name: Pinot Meunier
description: A key Champagne grape that brings ripe fruit, approachability, and early charm to sparkling blends. As a still wine it can show bright red berry flavors, supple texture, and moderate structure.

grapeCard.name: Pinot Meunier
grapeCard.info: A key Champagne grape that brings ripe fruit, approachability, and early charm to sparkling blends. As a still wine it can show bright red berry flavors, supple texture, and moderate structure.

name: Petite Sirah
description: A deeply colored, full-bodied red grape known for bold blackberry fruit, pepper, and firm tannins. Widely planted in California, it produces structured wines that can age well with oak integration.

grapeCard.name: Petite Sirah
grapeCard.info: A deeply colored, full-bodied red grape known for bold blackberry fruit, pepper, and firm tannins. Widely planted in California, it produces structured wines that can age well with oak integration.

name: Palomino
description: The principal white grape of Jerez, valued for its neutral profile and ability to transmit the effects of biological and oxidative aging in Sherry. In warm climates it yields dry wines with subtle orchard fruit, almond, and saline notes.

grapeCard.name: Palomino
grapeCard.info: The principal white grape of Jerez, valued for its neutral profile and ability to transmit the effects of biological and oxidative aging in Sherry. In warm climates it yields dry wines with subtle orchard fruit, almond, and saline notes.

name: Baga
description: Portugal's structured red grape from Bairrada, producing high-acid, high-tannin wines with dark berries, tobacco, and earthy complexity. It can be rustic in youth but rewards aging and careful extraction.

grapeCard.name: Baga
grapeCard.info: Portugal's structured red grape from Bairrada, producing high-acid, high-tannin wines with dark berries, tobacco, and earthy complexity. It can be rustic in youth but rewards aging and careful extraction.

name: Teroldego
description: An alpine red from Trentino known for vivid color, dark berry fruit, and firm but polished tannins. It combines mountain freshness with savory spice and often performs well in both stainless and oak-aged styles.

grapeCard.name: Teroldego
grapeCard.info: An alpine red from Trentino known for vivid color, dark berry fruit, and firm but polished tannins. It combines mountain freshness with savory spice and often performs well in both stainless and oak-aged styles.

name: Lagrein
description: A deeply pigmented grape from Alto Adige producing structured wines with blackberry, cocoa, and smoky spice. It can be intensely tannic when young, then evolves toward velvety, savory complexity.

grapeCard.name: Lagrein
grapeCard.info: A deeply pigmented grape from Alto Adige producing structured wines with blackberry, cocoa, and smoky spice. It can be intensely tannic when young, then evolves toward velvety, savory complexity.

name: Aligoté
description: Burgundy's bright, high-acid white grape offering lemon, green apple, and chalky mineral notes. Traditionally used in Kir and increasingly bottled as serious single-varietal wines from cooler limestone sites.

grapeCard.name: Aligoté
grapeCard.info: Burgundy's bright, high-acid white grape offering lemon, green apple, and chalky mineral notes. Traditionally used in Kir and increasingly bottled as serious single-varietal wines from cooler limestone sites.

name: Melon de Bourgogne
description: The classic grape of Muscadet, delivering lean, saline whites with citrus zest and subtle yeasty notes when aged on lees. Its neutral profile and brisk acidity make it especially seafood-friendly.

grapeCard.name: Melon de Bourgogne
grapeCard.info: The classic grape of Muscadet, delivering lean, saline whites with citrus zest and subtle yeasty notes when aged on lees. Its neutral profile and brisk acidity make it especially seafood-friendly.

name: Xinomavro
description: Greece's age-worthy northern red known for high acidity, firm tannins, and aromas of sour cherry, olive, and sun-dried tomato. Often compared stylistically to Nebbiolo for its structure and savory complexity.

grapeCard.name: Xinomavro
grapeCard.info: Greece's age-worthy northern red known for high acidity, firm tannins, and aromas of sour cherry, olive, and sun-dried tomato. Often compared stylistically to Nebbiolo for its structure and savory complexity.

name: Agiorgitiko
description: A versatile Greek red from Nemea producing plush wines with red berry fruit, warm spice, and supple tannins. Depending on altitude and élevage, styles range from juicy and youthful to rich and oak-influenced.

grapeCard.name: Agiorgitiko
grapeCard.info: A versatile Greek red from Nemea producing plush wines with red berry fruit, warm spice, and supple tannins. Depending on altitude and élevage, styles range from juicy and youthful to rich and oak-influenced.

name: Glera
description: The principal grape of Prosecco, prized for fresh pear, apple, and white blossom aromas with lively but gentle acidity. It is typically crafted in tank-method sparkling wines that emphasize bright fruit and approachability.

grapeCard.name: Glera
grapeCard.info: The principal grape of Prosecco, prized for fresh pear, apple, and white blossom aromas with lively but gentle acidity. It is typically crafted in tank-method sparkling wines that emphasize bright fruit and approachability.

name: Carignan
description: A Mediterranean red grape known for deep color, savory dark fruit, and naturally high acidity. Old-vine plantings in southern France and Spain can produce concentrated, mineral wines with notable structure.

grapeCard.name: Carignan
grapeCard.info: A Mediterranean red grape known for deep color, savory dark fruit, and naturally high acidity. Old-vine plantings in southern France and Spain can produce concentrated, mineral wines with notable structure.

name: Cinsault
description: A soft, aromatic red grape prized for red-fruit perfume and supple tannins. Often used in Rhône and southern French blends, it is also central to many pale, delicate rosé wines.

grapeCard.name: Cinsault
grapeCard.info: A soft, aromatic red grape prized for red-fruit perfume and supple tannins. Often used in Rhône and southern French blends, it is also central to many pale, delicate rosé wines.

name: Falanghina
description: A southern Italian white grape delivering bright citrus, apple, and floral aromas with refreshing acidity. It has become a flagship variety in Campania for both crisp young and textured styles.

grapeCard.name: Falanghina
grapeCard.info: A southern Italian white grape delivering bright citrus, apple, and floral aromas with refreshing acidity. It has become a flagship variety in Campania for both crisp young and textured styles.

name: Verdicchio
description: A classic central Italian white grape known for citrus zest, bitter-almond finish, and subtle saline minerality. It balances freshness and texture, with top bottlings capable of significant aging.

grapeCard.name: Verdicchio
grapeCard.info: A classic central Italian white grape known for citrus zest, bitter-almond finish, and subtle saline minerality. It balances freshness and texture, with top bottlings capable of significant aging.

name: Müller-Thurgau
description: A crossing of Riesling and Madeleine Royale that produces aromatic, easy-drinking whites with gentle acidity. Styles often show orchard fruit, fresh herbs, and soft floral notes.

grapeCard.name: Müller-Thurgau
grapeCard.info: A crossing of Riesling and Madeleine Royale that produces aromatic, easy-drinking whites with gentle acidity. Styles often show orchard fruit, fresh herbs, and soft floral notes.

name: Silvaner
description: A traditional Central European white grape offering understated aromatics, savory texture, and marked site expression. In Franconia and Alsace it can produce dry wines with herbal, orchard, and stony nuances.

grapeCard.name: Silvaner
grapeCard.info: A traditional Central European white grape offering understated aromatics, savory texture, and marked site expression. In Franconia and Alsace it can produce dry wines with herbal, orchard, and stony nuances.

name: Godello
description: A rising Spanish white grape from Galicia and Bierzo with citrus, orchard fruit, and mineral depth. It can be vinified in fresh steel styles or richer barrel-aged expressions with notable complexity.

grapeCard.name: Godello
grapeCard.info: A rising Spanish white grape from Galicia and Bierzo with citrus, orchard fruit, and mineral depth. It can be vinified in fresh steel styles or richer barrel-aged expressions with notable complexity.

name: Verdelho
description: A versatile white grape associated with Madeira and Australian coastal regions, showing tropical citrus, herbal hints, and bright freshness. It can produce both dry table wines and fortified expressions.

grapeCard.name: Verdelho
grapeCard.info: A versatile white grape associated with Madeira and Australian coastal regions, showing tropical citrus, herbal hints, and bright freshness. It can produce both dry table wines and fortified expressions.

name: Arinto
description: A Portuguese white grape prized for naturally high acidity and citrus-mineral precision, even in warm climates. It forms the backbone of many fresh Atlantic whites and ages gracefully with bottle time.

grapeCard.name: Arinto
grapeCard.info: A Portuguese white grape prized for naturally high acidity and citrus-mineral precision, even in warm climates. It forms the backbone of many fresh Atlantic whites and ages gracefully with bottle time.

name: Zweigelt
description: Austria's most planted red grape, combining juicy cherry fruit with peppery spice and bright acidity. It can range from fresh, everyday bottlings to more structured oak-aged versions.

grapeCard.name: Zweigelt
grapeCard.info: Austria's most planted red grape, combining juicy cherry fruit with peppery spice and bright acidity. It can range from fresh, everyday bottlings to more structured oak-aged versions.

name: Blaufränkisch
description: A central European red grape known for vivid acidity, dark berry fruit, and spicy, mineral edge. It produces energetic wines that can show excellent aging potential in cooler sites.

grapeCard.name: Blaufränkisch
grapeCard.info: A central European red grape known for vivid acidity, dark berry fruit, and spicy, mineral edge. It produces energetic wines that can show excellent aging potential in cooler sites.

name: Nerello Mascalese
description: Sicily's Etna flagship red, delivering pale color but firm structure with red fruit, herbs, and volcanic minerality. Often compared to Pinot Noir or Nebbiolo for its aromatic lift and tension.

grapeCard.name: Nerello Mascalese
grapeCard.info: Sicily's Etna flagship red, delivering pale color but firm structure with red fruit, herbs, and volcanic minerality. Often compared to Pinot Noir or Nebbiolo for its aromatic lift and tension.

name: Primitivo
description: A warm-climate red grape known for ripe black fruit, jammy richness, and generous alcohol. In Puglia it produces plush, spicy wines and shares a genetic identity with Zinfandel.

grapeCard.name: Primitivo
grapeCard.info: A warm-climate red grape known for ripe black fruit, jammy richness, and generous alcohol. In Puglia it produces plush, spicy wines and shares a genetic identity with Zinfandel.

name: Friulano
description: A northeastern Italian white grape with citrus, almond, and subtle herbal notes, often textured and dry. It is a benchmark variety of Friuli with notable food-pairing versatility.

grapeCard.name: Friulano
grapeCard.info: A northeastern Italian white grape with citrus, almond, and subtle herbal notes, often textured and dry. It is a benchmark variety of Friuli with notable food-pairing versatility.

name: Fer Servadou
description: A southwest French red grape valued for brisk acidity, black fruit, and peppery herbal character. It adds structure and freshness in blends and can produce savory, medium-bodied varietal wines.

grapeCard.name: Fer Servadou
grapeCard.info: A southwest French red grape valued for brisk acidity, black fruit, and peppery herbal character. It adds structure and freshness in blends and can produce savory, medium-bodied varietal wines.

name: Moschofilero
description: An aromatic Greek pink-skinned grape used for fragrant white wines with rose, citrus, and spice notes. It is especially associated with high-altitude Mantinia styles marked by freshness and perfume.

grapeCard.name: Moschofilero
grapeCard.info: An aromatic Greek pink-skinned grape used for fragrant white wines with rose, citrus, and spice notes. It is especially associated with high-altitude Mantinia styles marked by freshness and perfume.

name: Bordeaux
description: The world's most famous wine region, renowned for its prestigious blends of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Its classification system and château culture have set the standard for fine wine globally.

name: Burgundy
description: The spiritual home of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, where terroir is expressed with unmatched precision. Its complex hierarchy of vineyards spans from regional wines to legendary Grand Crus.

name: Champagne
description: The only region that can legally produce true Champagne, using the traditional method of secondary fermentation in bottle. Its chalky soils and cool climate create wines of exceptional finesse and elegance.

name: Rhône Valley
description: A sun-drenched region divided into the elegant Northern Rhône and the broader Southern Rhône. It produces powerful Syrahs in the north and rich GSM blends in the south.

name: Loire Valley
description: Known as the Garden of France, this diverse region stretches along the Loire River. It produces everything from crisp Sauvignon Blanc to age-worthy Chenin Blanc and elegant Cabernet Franc.

name: Alsace
description: A unique French region with Germanic influence, known for its aromatic white wines in distinctive tall bottles. The dry Rieslings and spicy Gewürztraminers are among France's finest whites.

name: Provence
description: The world's most famous rosé-producing region, blessed with Mediterranean sunshine and maritime breezes. Its pale, dry rosés have become synonymous with summer elegance.

name: Beaujolais
description: The home of Gamay grape, producing bright, fruity wines that range from simple Nouveau to age-worthy Cru wines. Its ten crus showcase the diversity possible from a single grape variety.

name: Languedoc
description: France's largest wine region, offering exceptional value and increasingly high quality wines. Once known for bulk wine, it now produces exciting reds from Mediterranean varieties.

name: Chablis
description: A northern outpost of Burgundy famous for mineral, unoaked Chardonnay that expresses pure chalky terroir. Its Grand Crus and Premier Crus are among the world's greatest white wines.

name: Sauternes
description: The world's most celebrated sweet wine region, where noble rot transforms Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc into liquid gold. Château d'Yquem leads this prestigious appellation.

name: Jura
description: A quirky French region known for its unique oxidative wines like Vin Jaune and distinctive local grapes. Its Savagnin and Poulsard wines offer flavors found nowhere else.

name: Napa Valley
description: America's most prestigious wine region, known for powerful Cabernet Sauvignon that rivals Bordeaux. Its combination of climate, terroir, and investment has created world-class wines.

name: Sonoma
description: Napa's diverse coastal neighbor, producing excellent wines from numerous varieties across varied microclimates. Its cooler sites excel with Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.
climateDescription: Pacific fog and breezes temper ripening.

name: Willamette Valley
description: Oregon's cool-climate paradise for Pinot Noir, producing elegant wines that rival Burgundy. Its diverse soil types and microclimates create wines of remarkable complexity.

name: Paso Robles
description: California's hub for Rhône varieties, producing bold Syrahs and GSM blends in a warm, inland climate. Its limestone soils add complexity to powerful, ripe wines.

name: Walla Walla
description: Washington State's premium wine valley, known for elegant Syrah and refined Cabernet Sauvignon. Its high desert climate produces wines of intense flavor and balance.

name: Santa Barbara
description: Cool-climate California wine country made famous by the film Sideways. Its transverse valleys channel ocean fog to create ideal conditions for Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.
climateDescription: Ocean-cooled valleys with strong diurnal shift.

name: Finger Lakes
description: New York State's premier wine region, producing world-class Riesling from steep lakeside vineyards. The deep lakes moderate the harsh climate to allow viticulture.

name: Lodi
description: California's treasure trove of old vine Zinfandel, some over 100 years old. These ancient vines produce concentrated, complex wines at excellent value.

name: Tuscany
description: Italy's most beloved wine region, where Sangiovese reigns supreme across varied terroirs. From Chianti to Brunello to Super Tuscans, it offers incredible diversity.

name: Piedmont
description: The home of Nebbiolo, producing Italy's greatest red wines including Barolo and Barbaresco. Its fog-shrouded hills also yield excellent Barbera and Moscato.

name: Veneto
description: Italy's most productive wine region, home to Prosecco, Amarone, and Soave. Its diverse wines range from light sparklers to powerful dried-grape reds.

name: Sicily
description: Italy's largest wine island, producing increasingly impressive wines from ancient native varieties. Volcanic Mount Etna has become a hotbed for elegant reds and mineral whites.

name: Campania
description: Southern Italy's jewel of ancient grape varieties grown on volcanic soils near Naples. Aglianico produces powerful reds while Fiano and Greco make distinctive whites.

name: Friuli-Venezia Giulia
description: Italy's top region for premium white wines, bordering Slovenia and Austria. Its mineral-driven Friulano and aromatic Ribolla Gialla showcase northeastern elegance.

name: Alto Adige
description: A bilingual Italian-German region in the Alps producing fresh, aromatic white wines. Its steep mountain vineyards yield distinctive Pinot Grigio and Gewürztraminer.

name: Abruzzo
description: Central Italy's workhorse region, famous for value-driven Montepulciano d'Abruzzo. Its rustic, fruity reds offer excellent quality at accessible prices.

name: Puglia
description: The heel of Italy's boot, producing bold, fruity reds from Primitivo and Negroamaro. Once a source of blending wine, it now bottles excellent value wines.

name: Rioja
description: Spain's most famous wine region, known for Tempranillo-based wines aged in American oak. Its traditional wines offer vanilla and coconut notes alongside red fruit.

name: Ribera del Duero
description: High-altitude wine country producing powerful, concentrated Tempranillo wines. Its extreme climate creates bold wines with intense fruit and firm structure.

name: Priorat
description: A dramatic region of ancient slate soils producing intensely concentrated wines. Its Garnacha and Cariñena vines struggle in poor soil to yield powerful, mineral wines.

name: Rías Baixas
description: Spain's premier white wine region on the Atlantic coast of Galicia. Its Albariño wines show distinctive salinity and freshness from the maritime influence.

name: Rueda
description: Spain's answer to Sauvignon Blanc country, producing crisp, herbal whites from Verdejo. Its limestone soils and continental climate create refreshing wines.

name: Jerez
description: The birthplace of Sherry, producing unique fortified wines through solera aging. From dry Fino to sweet Pedro Ximénez, it offers unmatched complexity.

name: Douro
description: Portugal's great river valley, home to Port wine and increasingly impressive dry reds. Its terraced schist hillsides are a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Vinho Verde
description: Portugal's refreshing white wine region producing light, crisp wines with a slight spritz. Its cool, rainy climate creates wines perfect for seafood.

name: Mosel
description: Germany's most famous wine region, producing ethereal Rieslings from impossibly steep slate slopes. Its light, aromatic wines balance sweetness with electric acidity.

name: Rheingau
description: A historic German region where noble Riesling was first cultivated for dry wines. Its south-facing slopes along the Rhine produce full-bodied, age-worthy whites.

name: Wachau
description: Austria's most prestigious wine region along the Danube River, producing world-class Riesling and Grüner Veltliner. Its terraced vineyards date back centuries.

name: Barossa Valley
description: Australia's most famous wine region, home to century-old Shiraz vines producing powerful, rich wines. Its warm climate and ancient soils create wines of incredible concentration.

name: Margaret River
description: Western Australia's premium region producing Bordeaux-style blends and elegant Chardonnay. Its maritime climate creates wines of exceptional balance and refinement.

name: Marlborough
description: New Zealand's largest and most famous region, producing intensely aromatic Sauvignon Blanc. Its wines have redefined the grape worldwide with their pungent tropical character.

name: Central Otago
description: The world's southernmost wine region, producing exceptional Pinot Noir at high altitude. Its dramatic landscape yields wines of remarkable purity and intensity.

name: Mendoza
description: Argentina's wine heartland at the foot of the Andes, where high-altitude vineyards produce world-class Malbec. Its sunny days and cool nights create wines of intense color and flavor.

name: Maipo Valley
description: Chile's most prestigious wine valley, known for powerful Cabernet Sauvignon. Its proximity to Santiago and excellent terroir make it Chile's wine showcase.

name: Stellenbosch
description: South Africa's premier wine region, producing world-class Cabernet Sauvignon and unique Pinotage. Its mountain slopes and varied soils create wines of great diversity.

name: Santorini
description: A Greek island of volcanic vineyards producing intensely mineral Assyrtiko. Its ancient vines are trained in basket shapes to protect against fierce winds.

name: Tokaj
description: Hungary's legendary region producing Tokaji Aszú, one of the world's great sweet wines. Its botrytis-affected Furmint grapes create wines of extraordinary complexity.

name: Helan Mountain
description: China's flagship high-desert wine frontier in Ningxia, producing polished Bordeaux-style reds under dramatic rocky ridges.

name: Shangri-La
description: Sky-high vineyards perched along the Mekong in Yunnan, delivering perfumed reds with alpine freshness from ancient terraces.

name: Yamanashi
description: Japan's Kōshu Valley beneath Mount Fuji, home to delicate Koshu whites and experimental sparkling wines with pristine acidity.

name: Nashik
description: India's wine capital north of Mumbai, where breezy plateaus yield tropical-leaning whites and peppery Syrah.

name: Nandi Hills
description: Cooler Karnataka hill country that lends lift and spice to India's premium Syrah and Cabernet bottlings.

name: Swartland
description: Swartland's old bush vines and granite soils craft textured Chenin Blanc and soulful Rhône-style reds with minimal intervention.

name: Walker Bay
description: A cool, ocean-swept corner famed for Hemel-en-Aarde Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, delivering Burgundian elegance with South African sunshine.

name: Paarl & Franschhoek
description: Historic valleys east of Stellenbosch producing rich reds, fynbos-scented whites, and South Africa's marquee Cap Classique sparklers.

name: Kakheti
description: Georgia's eastern cradle of wine, famous for skin-contact amber wines and powerful Saperavi reds fermented in buried qvevri.

name: Kartli
description: Highland vineyards near Tbilisi yielding elegant sparkling Chinuri and balanced qvevri whites with alpine freshness.

name: Imereti
description: Western Georgia's humid hills where partial qvevri fermentations craft brighter, mineral-driven amber and red wines.

name: Full-Bodied Red
description: Rich, tannic wines with intense dark fruit flavors that command attention on the palate. These powerful wines typically benefit from aging and pair beautifully with hearty red meats.

name: Light-Bodied Red
description: Delicate, elegant reds with bright acidity and fresh red fruit character that are perfect for lighter fare. These subtle wines showcase finesse over power and can even be served slightly chilled.

name: Medium-Bodied Red
description: Versatile reds that balance fruit, structure, and elegance for everyday enjoyment. These food-friendly wines bridge the gap between light and bold styles.

name: Full-Bodied White
description: Opulent white wines often with oak influence, offering rich texture and creamy mouthfeel. These wines pair wonderfully with rich seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Light-Bodied White
description: Crisp, refreshing whites with high acidity and clean, mineral-driven flavors. These thirst-quenching wines are perfect aperitifs or paired with light seafood.

name: Aromatic White
description: Intensely fragrant white wines with floral, fruity, and spicy aromas that leap from the glass. These expressive wines often have a touch of residual sugar to balance their bold aromatics.

name: Rosé
description: Refreshing pink wines made from red grapes with minimal skin contact. These versatile, food-friendly wines range from bone-dry to slightly sweet.

name: Sparkling Wine
description: Effervescent wines with elegant bubbles created through secondary fermentation. From simple Prosecco to prestigious Champagne, bubbles add celebration to any occasion.

name: Champagne
description: The original and most prestigious sparkling wine, made only in the Champagne region of France. Its traditional method production creates unmatched finesse and complexity.

name: Dessert Wine
description: Lusciously sweet wines crafted from super-ripe or dried grapes, perfect for ending a meal. These concentrated wines balance intense sweetness with vibrant acidity.

name: Fortified Wine
description: Wines strengthened with grape spirit to increase alcohol and preserve sweetness. From dry Sherry to sweet Port, these complex wines offer remarkable longevity.

name: Port
description: Portugal's famous sweet fortified wine from the Douro Valley, made by adding brandy during fermentation. Its rich, concentrated flavors range from fruity Ruby to aged Tawny styles.

name: Sherry
description: Spain's unique fortified wine from Jerez, aged in a solera system to create complex oxidative or biological styles. From dry Fino to sweet Pedro Ximénez, it offers incredible diversity.

name: Natural Wine
description: Low-intervention wines made with minimal additives and often without filtration. These authentic wines showcase the true expression of grape and terroir.

name: Orange Wine
description: White grapes vinified like red wine, with extended skin contact creating amber color and tannic structure. This ancient technique produces textural, complex wines.

name: Pétillant Naturel
description: Naturally sparkling wines made using the ancestral method, bottled before primary fermentation completes. These rustic, cloudy sparklers offer fresh, unpredictable character.

name: Late Harvest
description: Sweet wines made from grapes left on the vine past normal harvest to concentrate sugar. This simple technique creates rich, fruity dessert wines without noble rot.

name: Ice Wine
description: Ultra-sweet wines made from grapes naturally frozen on the vine, concentrating sugars and acids. These rare, expensive wines require specific climate conditions to produce.

name: Botrytis Wine
description: Noble rot sweet wines where Botrytis cinerea fungus concentrates grape sugars while adding honeyed complexity. Sauternes and Tokaji are the most famous examples.

name: GSM Blend
description: Classic Rhône-style blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre creating complex, balanced reds. Each grape contributes fruit, structure, and aromatics to the final wine.

name: Bordeaux Blend
description: Classic blend dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with supporting varieties. This noble style has been replicated worldwide for its structure and aging potential.

name: Super Tuscan
description: Non-traditional Italian wines that broke from DOC rules by blending international varieties with local grapes. These innovative wines put modern Italian winemaking on the map.

name: Cru Beaujolais
description: Age-worthy Gamay from the ten named villages of Beaujolais, offering serious wines beyond simple Nouveau. Each cru has distinct character from its unique granite-based terroir.

name: Crémant
description: French sparkling wines made in the traditional method outside Champagne, offering excellent value. Crémant d'Alsace, Loire, and Burgundy are the most notable examples.

name: Prosecco
description: Italy's most popular sparkling wine, made from Glera grapes using the tank method. Its fresh, fruity character and gentle bubbles make it perfect for casual celebrations.

name: Qvevri Amber
description: Skin-contact whites fermented in buried clay qvevri, yielding tea-like tannin, dried orchard fruit, and honeyed spice.

name: Fresh Chillable Red
description: Light, low-tannin reds built for a chill, bursting with red fruit and peppery zest for warm-weather sipping.

name: Sparkling Red
description: Fizz meets dark fruit: from Lambrusco to sparkling Shiraz, these wines marry bubbles with berry-rich, savory depth.

name: Noble Grapes
description: A curated grouping of the NOBLE-tier varieties whose pedigree, structure, and complexity define the most prestigious styles.
